name: Gaida (Rhodope Bagpipe)
description: The gaida is the soul of Rhodope music. Made from a goat stomach and hand-carved chanter, it produces the haunting sound that defines the region. Shiroka Laka has the only remaining bagpipe school in Bulgaria.

name: Nestinarstvo (Fire-Walking)
description: On the feast of Sts. Constantine and Helena, Nestinari enter a trance state and walk barefoot on live coals carrying sacred icons. The ritual is pre-Christian, over 3000 years old, and survives only in Strandzha.

name: Kukeri (Mummer Ritual)
description: Men dress in enormous fur and bell costumes to drive away evil spirits in late winter. The bells weigh up to 30kg. The ritual predates written Bulgarian history.

name: Trevnenska School of Icon Painting
description: The Tryavna school of icon painting developed its own distinctive style in the 17th century — flat gold backgrounds, elongated figures, intense dark eyes. Works in the Rila Monastery and across the Balkans.

name: Tryavna School of Woodcarving
description: A distinct woodcarving tradition developed in Tryavna in the 18th century. Master craftsmen traveled across the Ottoman Empire decorating mosques, churches, and konak halls. The craft is now maintained by fewer than 20 active masters.

name: Lazaruvane (Girls' Spring Ritual)
description: On Lazarus Saturday, young women dress in traditional costumes and go house to house singing specific songs for each family member. One of the oldest surviving spring fertility rituals in the Balkans.

name: Bulgarian Yogurt Culture
description: Lactobacillus bulgaricus, the bacterium that makes Bulgarian yogurt unique, was isolated in 1905 from a sample taken near Teteven. The same culture has been passed down unbroken for centuries. EU protected designation of origin.

name: Rosa Damascena Oil Production
description: Bulgaria produces 70% of the world's rose oil. The copper pot distillation method used in the Kazanlak valley and surrounding foothills is unchanged since the 17th century. Three tonnes of petals yield one kilogram of oil.

name: Horo (Chain Dance)
description: The horo is the oldest Bulgarian dance form, performed in a chain or circle. Each region has its own rhythm — the Rhodope horo is slow and melancholic. Still performed at every village celebration.

name: April Uprising Memory Culture
description: The 1876 April Uprising against Ottoman rule began in Koprivshtitsa. Its suppression led directly to the Russo-Turkish War and Bulgarian liberation. The memory is kept alive through living history, house-museums, and annual reenactments.
